source_jobs.title: Developer Advocate
source_jobs.description:
About Sent

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.

We've raised over $5M in Seed funding backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments. We're hiring to build the best infrastructure to replace multi-channel messaging stacks.

The Role

We're hiring a Developer advocate to be the technical voice of Sent among developers. The job is to produce a steady stream of technical content, code, demos, posts, videos, talks, and to engage with developers where they actually hang out: Hacker News, X, Reddit, Discord servers, GitHub.

You'll write the tutorial that ranks for "how to send WhatsApp from Node.js." You'll attend events, conferences, hackathons, and host some in-house. You'll build the demo that gets passed around in a Slack channel. You'll be in the comments when someone asks how Sent compares to Twilio, and you'll have a useful answer ready.


What you'll do

 - Publish technical content on a regular cadence: tutorials, comparison posts, deep dives, working demos, sample apps

 - Maintain a strong personal presence on X, YouTube, Linkedin, other dev media channels, building an audience of developers who care about messaging infrastructure

 - Show up at developer conferences and meetups, sometimes speaking, sometimes running a booth, sometimes both

 - Host and co-host events and strategic hackathons, embedding Sent in the dev ecosystem

 - Own the SDK and quickstart developer experience end to end, and feed friction back to engineering

 - Run office hours, livestreams, or whatever format works to get builders engaged with the product
   

What you bring

 - At least 4 years working as a developer, with at least 1-2 of those years in DevRel, developer marketing, or as a public-facing technical IC

 - You ship code. You can build a working integration in a weekend and write up how you did it on Monday

 - A real portfolio of public technical content: blog posts, repos, talks, videos, or a following you've built yourself

 - Strong writing. You can explain a webhook signature flow without making the reader want to die

 - Comfort with messaging, telecom, or API infrastructure is a plus but not required

 

Our Commitment to You

 - Meaningful equity with high growth potential

 - Top-tier medical, dental, vision fully covered

 - 401(k) with 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful NYC workspace with daily meals and unlimited snacks

 - New MacBook Pro + Apple Studio Display

 - Wellhub Gold or Equinox membership stipend

Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Technical Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SENT

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.




THE ROLE

You own integration support and go-live for contracted customers, from signed deal through onboarding, first production traffic, and the full lifecycle after. You'll get customers live, keep them healthy, and grow them. That requires enough technical depth to debug failed sends, write integration code, design messaging architectures, and explain carrier behavior without relying on engineering for every issue.

This is as much a coding role as an account role. When an integration stalls, you open the editor. You'll review customer code, write working examples, reproduce issues against our API, and work directly with customer engineers. Contracted, sales-led accounts are your primary focus, with select high-potential self-serve accounts as they scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own onboarding and go-live for contracted customers: scope use cases, design integrations (REST APIs, webhooks, sender setup), and launch across 10DLC/TCR, WhatsApp, toll-free, and short code.

 - Review customer code, build sample integrations and test scripts, reproduce issues, and pair with customer engineers when implementation work stalls.

 - Act as the customer's technical point of contact for routing, throughput, rate limits, encoding, segmentation, delivery receipts, and channel fallback.

 - Diagnose deliverability and integration issues across the stack, tracing messages from API request through carrier DLR and identifying root causes.

 - Advise on resilient, compliant, and cost-effective messaging architectures, including sender strategy, retry logic, idempotency, fallback, and opt-out handling.

 - Run QBRs, review performance and goals, surface insights, and recommend optimizations.

 - Drive expansion by identifying growth opportunities and partnering with sales on larger accounts.

 - Serve as a high-context bridge to engineering and product, translating customer needs into actionable feedback.

 - Build repeatable onboarding and account-management processes as the function grows.


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - Experience as a Technical Account Manager or similar solutions, implementation, or technical CSM role in telecom, CPaaS, or messaging.

 - Strong software engineering skills. You can build integrations against REST APIs, stand up webhook receivers, write scripts, and troubleshoot unfamiliar codebases. Explaining documentation is not enough. You should be comfortable building the integration yourself.

 - Solid understanding of messaging infrastructure: carrier routing, SMPP, DLRs, throughput, queuing, encoding, segmentation, channel fallback, and event-driven architectures.

 - Experience with 10DLC/TCR registration, WhatsApp Business Accounts and templates, toll-free verification, and short codes.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to communicate clearly with both developers and business stakeholders.

 - Ownership mindset and comfort building process in a fast-moving environment.


A NOTE ON FIT: THIS IS A TECHNICAL ROLE WITH CUSTOMER OWNERSHIP ATTACHED. IF YOU'VE TYPICALLY HANDED OFF IMPLEMENTATION WORK TO SOLUTIONS ENGINEERS, IT'S PROBABLY NOT A FIT. IF YOU'VE TAKEN CUSTOMERS FROM SIGNED CONTRACT TO FIRST PRODUCTION SEND YOURSELF, YOU'LL KNOW EXACTLY WHAT THIS JOB ENTAILS.




OUR COMMITMENT TO YOU

 - Meaningful equity with high growth potential

 - Competitive compensation - flexible with years and depth of experience

 - Top-tier medical, dental, vision fully covered

 - 401(k) with 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful NYC workspace with daily meals and unlimited snacks

 - New MacBook Pro + Apple Studio Display

 - Wellhub Gold or Equinox membership

Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer commit

source_jobs.title: Technical Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Pristina, Kosovo · In-Office



About Sent

Sent is building the messaging infrastructure that powers how businesses communicate with their customers. We provide a unified API for SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and RCS, making it simple for developers to reach people on any channel. We've raised $5.8M in funding and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for a Technical Support Engineer to be the first line of defense for developers and businesses building on Sent. You'll resolve technical issues, guide customers through complex integrations, and make sure they're successful on the platform.



What You'll Do

• Resolve technical issues. Help customers debug integration problems, diagnose API errors, and troubleshoot messaging failures across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS.

• Own the customer experience. Be the person customers rely on when they hit a problem. Respond quickly, communicate clearly, and follow through.

• Escalate effectively. Know when to bring in engineering. Write clear, reproducible issue summaries so engineers can move fast.

• Build internal knowledge. Document recurring issues, write support guides, and help build the systems that make the team more effective over time.

• Surface product feedback. Sit at the intersection of customers and product. Identify patterns and advocate internally for fixes and improvements.



What We're Looking For

• 3+ years in technical support, ideally in SaaS, tech, telecom, or fintech. You've supported developers or technical users and aren't afraid of a deep dive.

• Excellent spoken and written English. You communicate with precision and professionalism.

• Technical aptitude. You understand APIs, can read logs, and are comfortable troubleshooting platform-level issues.

• Strong ownership. You follow problems through to resolution. You don't pass the buck.

• Experience handling technical issues, escalations, or platform-related inquiries is a plus. Bonus if you've worked in a messaging or telecom context.



Why Sent

We're a team building hard technical problems in messaging infrastructure. We value directness, craftsmanship, and actually shipping things. In Pristina, you'll be part of a close-knit engineering and operations team doing real work — not support work, not maintenance work. Work that matters. If you want to join a fast-moving company with a clear product and strong backing, this is that opportunity.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Pristina, Kosovo · In-Office

About Sent

Sent is building the messaging infrastructure that powers how businesses communicate with their customers. We provide a unified API for SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and RCS, making it simple for developers to reach people on any channel. We've raised $5.8M in funding and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for a Full Stack Engineer who can move across the entire product — designing APIs and shipping UI with equal confidence. You'll take ownership of end-to-end features that touch every layer of the Sent platform.



What You'll Do

• Own features end-to-end. Take requirements from design to delivery — backend API, frontend UI, database, and everything in between.

• Work across both stacks. Build and maintain services in .NET / ASP.NET Core on the backend and Next.js / React on the frontend. Comfortable in both without being precious about either.

• Integrate with the broader system. Connect to carrier networks, internal services, and third-party APIs. Understand how your features fit into the larger architecture.

• Move fast without breaking things. Ship regularly and take responsibility for what you build. Write tests, observe your deployments, and fix what breaks.

• Bring a product mindset. Think about what you're building and why. Push back when something doesn't make sense and suggest better approaches when you see them.

What We're Looking For

• 3+ years across both frontend and backend development. Not a preference — you actually do both.

• Hands-on Next.js / React and .NET / ASP.NET Core. You've shipped real features in both stacks.

• Experience with APIs, databases, and integrations. You understand how systems connect and what it takes to make them reliable.

• Scalability awareness. You think about what happens when load increases before it happens.

• Strong product instincts. You're not just building what's on the ticket. You're building the right thing.

Why Sent

We're a team building hard technical problems in messaging infrastructure. We value directness, craftsmanship, and actually shipping things. In Pristina, you'll be part of a close-knit engineering and operations team doing real work — not support work, not maintenance work. Work that matters. If you want to join a fast-moving company with a clear product and strong backing, this is that opportunity.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Automation Tester
source_jobs.description:
Pristina, Kosovo · In-Office

About Sent

Sent is building the messaging infrastructure that powers how businesses communicate with their customers. We provide a unified API for SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and RCS, making it simple for developers to reach people on any channel. We've raised $5.8M in funding and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for an Automation Tester to help ensure that Sent's platform ships reliably. You'll build and maintain automated test suites, work closely with engineering, and be a meaningful voice on quality across the product.



What You'll Do

• Build automated test coverage. Write and maintain automated tests across web, API, and end-to-end product flows. Build suites that are maintainable and actually catch real bugs.

• Own regression testing. Maintain and expand regression suites as the product grows. Catch issues before they reach production.

• Work alongside engineering. Embed with engineering teams in agile sprints. Review features before they ship and flag quality issues early.

• Track and communicate defects. Document bugs clearly, prioritize with engineering, and verify fixes. Keep defect tracking clean and actionable.

• Improve testing infrastructure. Identify gaps in coverage, propose improvements to test architecture, and help raise the quality bar across the codebase.



What We're Looking For

• 3+ years in QA and test automation. You've built and maintained automated test suites in real production environments.

• Hands-on with automation frameworks. You're comfortable writing tests in at least one major automation framework and choosing the right tool for the job.

• Web and API testing experience. You've tested both frontend applications and backend APIs. End-to-end testing is part of your toolkit.

• Strong understanding of testing methodologies. You know when to use unit, integration, and E2E tests — and how to balance them.

• Agile experience. You're used to fast-moving development cycles and embedding quality into the process from the start.



Why Sent

We're a team building hard technical problems in messaging infrastructure. We value directness, craftsmanship, and actually shipping things. In Pristina, you'll be part of a close-knit engineering and operations team doing real work — not support work, not maintenance work. Work that matters. If you want to join a fast-moving company with a clear product and strong backing, this is that opportunity.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Founding Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
About Sent

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.

We recently closed a $5.1M Seed round backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments. We're hiring to build the best infrastructure to replace multi-channel messaging stacks.

About the Role

We're hiring someone to build Sent's partner ecosystem across the startups and developer tools landscape. You'll own the full lifecycle - identifying high-leverage partnerships, structuring agreements, launching integrations, co-hosting events, and driving co-sell revenue.

Because Sent is an API-first product, this isn't a role where you can hand-wave the technical details. You need to understand how developers evaluate and adopt infrastructure, speak credibly about APIs and integrations, and ideally have built things yourself. The best version of this person has spent time in tech and still ships side projects on weekends.

This is a build-from-scratch role. You'll define the strategy, close the first wave of partnerships, then operationalize what works into scalable programs as Sent grows.

What You'll Do

Strategic & Integration Partnerships

 - Identify and prioritize partnerships across developer tools, CRMs, marketing platforms, and marketplaces based on revenue potential and integration value

 - Structure partnership agreements - co-sell, referral, integration, and reseller models

 - Negotiate commercial terms, SLAs, and GTM commitments

 - Coordinate with product and engineering on integration roadmaps and technical requirements

Ecosystem & GTM

 - Launch co-marketing and co-sell programs that generate measurable pipeline

 - Host in-person events and represent Sent at partner events, conferences, and industry forums

 - Track partner-sourced revenue, integration adoption, and ecosystem health metrics

 - Report performance to leadership and shape broader GTM strategy

What You'll Bring

Required

 - At least 4 years in partnerships/BD/sales at a developer-focused company or VC (investing, platform, or portfolio ops)

 - Experience structuring and closing partnership agreements - not just sourcing

 - Strong understanding of API ecosystems, SaaS business models, and how developers adopt infrastructure

 - Technically conversational - you can demo an API, read docs, and talk integration architecture with engineers

 - Proven ability to build programs that generate measurable revenue or adoption

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with product, engineering, and sales

 - Excellent negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills

 - Located in NYC or willing to relocate

Nice to Have

 - You build side projects, hack on APIs, or have shipped something yourself

 - Previous experience building a partnerships function from scratch

 - Existing network across VC platform teams and/or the developer tools ecosystem

 - Familiarity with messaging APIs, CPaaS, or communications infrastructure

What Makes This Role Special

 - Own the function. Build Sent's partnerships org from strategy to execution - this is your team to build.

 - Massive surface area. Messaging touches every SaaS company, CRM, and communication platform. The partner ecosystem is enormous.

 - Native distribution. Use your network as a real go-to-market channel,

 - Revenue impact. Partnerships directly drive adoption, integration depth, and deal size. Your work shows up in the numbers.

 - Cross-functional influence. Shape product roadmap through partner requirements and integration priorities.

Our Commitment to You

 - Meaningful equity with high growth potential

 - Top-tier medical, dental, vision fully covered

 - 401(k) with 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful NYC workspace with daily meals and unl

source_jobs.title: Growth Advisor
source_jobs.description:
About Sent

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.

We recently closed a $5.1M Seed round backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments. We're hiring to build the best infrastructure to replace multi-channel messaging stacks.

About the Role

We're hiring a Startups Growth Advisor to identify businesses of all stages evaluating Sent. You'll be the first conversation founders, product teams, and CTOs have with us, helping them understand how intelligent message routing fits into what they're building - whether that's transactional notifications, authentication flows, or customer engagement across channels.

You'll spend your time finding companies that use messaging infrastructure, starting conversations with product teams and CTOs, and guiding them through an initial evaluation of Sent. When a company is ready for a demo, you'll connect them with the right people on our team and make sure nothing falls through the cracks.

This role is perfect for someone early in their career who's genuinely curious about developer tools, enjoys talking to people building things, and wants to be at a company where the product sells itself once people see it.

What You'll Do

Pipeline Development

 - Identify and engage new verticals for companies building messaging infrastructure

 - Run outbound outreach through email, LinkedIn, and developer communities

 - Qualify inbound interest from marketing, content, and product signups

 - Research target companies using tools and product signals to personalize every conversation

Technical Discovery

 - Run first conversations with technical founders and early engineering teams to understand their messaging needs

 - Explain Sent's intelligent routing, channel coverage, and pricing model at a conversational level

 - Assess fit based on volume, use case, channel requirements, and timeline

Coordination & Follow-Through

 - Connect qualified companies with GTM Engineers and senior team members for technical evaluations

 - Maintain clean records in CRM with thorough notes and accurate pipeline data

 - Provide weekly reporting on pipeline activity and conversion

What You'll Bring

Required

 - 0–2 years of experience in a customer-facing or commercial role

 - Strong written and verbal communication - your outreach needs to land with technical founders

 - Genuine curiosity about developer tools, APIs, and how startups build products

 - Organized and disciplined with follow-up and data hygiene

 - Comfortable with high-volume outreach, cold-calling, proactive relationship-building

 - Self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, ambiguous startup environments

 - Located in NYC or willing to move

Nice to Have

 - Experience at an API-first, developer tools, or infrastructure company

 - Comfort reading API documentation or understanding basic integration concepts

 - Experience with CRM and outreach tools

What Makes This Role Special

 - Advisory Positioning: You're helping startups make smart infrastructure decisions. Your credibility compounds with every conversation.

 - Ground Floor: Help define how Sent engages early-stage companies at a company that just closed $5.1M and is scaling fast.

 - Clear Growth Path: As you build expertise in the product and market, you'll grow into broader commercial roles.

Our Commitment to You

 - Meaningful equity with high growth potential

 - Competitive salary + uncapped commission

 - Top-tier medical, dental, vision fully covered

 - 401(k) with 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful NYC workspace with daily meals and unlimited snacks

 - New MacBook Pro + Apple Studio Display

 - Wellhub Gold or Equinox stipend

Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to b

source_jobs.title: Frontend Engineer (Next.js)
source_jobs.description:
Pristina, Kosovo · In-Office

About Sent

Sent is building the messaging infrastructure that powers how businesses communicate with their customers. We provide a unified API for SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and RCS, making it simple for developers to reach people on any channel. We've raised $5.8M in funding and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for a Frontend Engineer to build the interfaces developers and businesses use to interact with Sent. From dashboards to onboarding flows, you'll shape how customers experience the platform.



What You'll Do

• Build the product UI. Own and ship frontend features across Sent's web platform — dashboards, settings, onboarding, and more. Write clean, reusable components that work across devices.

• Hold a high performance bar. Optimize for load times, rendering performance, and responsiveness. Users notice when things are slow.

• Work closely with design and backend. Translate designs into production-ready code and collaborate with backend engineers to integrate APIs correctly.

• Own the component architecture. Help define how components are structured, shared, and maintained across the codebase.

• Ship fast. We move quickly. You'll be comfortable delivering features in a fast-moving environment without sacrificing quality.

What We're Looking For

• 3+ years of frontend development experience. You've worked in production Next.js or React environments and know how to ship things that hold up.

• Strong expertise in JavaScript / TypeScript. You write typed code by default and understand why it matters.

• State management and API integration. You know how to handle complex state and connect frontend applications to backend APIs cleanly.

• Eye for design and UX. You care about how things look and feel, not just whether they work.

• Experience building production-ready web applications in fast-moving environments. You've shipped and maintained real applications under real constraints.



Why Sent

We're a team building hard technical problems in messaging infrastructure. We value directness, craftsmanship, and actually shipping things. In Pristina, you'll be part of a close-knit engineering and operations team doing real work — not support work, not maintenance work. Work that matters. If you want to join a fast-moving company with a clear product and strong backing, this is that opportunity.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Messaging Compliance Manager, International and WhatsApp
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As Messaging Compliance Manager specialized in International, WhatsApp & RCS at Sent, you'll own the compliance work that keeps customers sending across borders, on WhatsApp, and on RCS.

Sent is a developer-first company powering messaging across SMS, WhatsApp, RCS, and more. Compliance isn't an afterthought here - it's how the product works and how customers trust us to deliver. This role has immediate, high-volume impact, and it's a hands-on operator role: from your first weeks you'll be carrying live registrations, template reviews, and agent launches, not writing policy from the sidelines. You'll work alongside our US-focused compliance lead, sharing context so both regions stay fully covered.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL…

 - Own WhatsApp compliance end to end: template creation, categorization (marketing, utility, authentication), and review; troubleshooting template rejections and quality-rating issues; display name and sender approvals; and navigating Meta processes so customers can launch and scale.

 - Own RCS agent compliance end to end: agent setup and brand verification, carrier and regional launch approvals, use-case categorization, and content and consent policy review - navigating Google RBM and carrier processes so customers can launch verified agents and keep them in good standing.

 - Own international sender registration and country-by-country requirements, including sender ID registration, required documentation (country-specific forms, stamps, attestations), number type selection, and pre-registration where carriers or regulators require it.

 - Be the go-to expert on international messaging rules by region, advising customers and internal teams on exactly what's needed to send compliantly in a given country.

 - Partner with our US compliance lead to provide tag-team coverage across US and international, cross-training so each region has a backup.

 - Work directly with vendors, carriers, and aggregators on registration requirements, escalations, and policy changes that affect deliverability.

 - Support customers, sales, and the support team on international, WhatsApp, and RCS compliance questions, turning dense carrier and regulatory rules into clear, practical guidance.

 - Maintain internal documentation and playbooks so international, WhatsApp, and RCS compliance stays repeatable as we scale.

 - Track registrations, template approvals and declines, RCS agent launches, and compliance issues, and surface trends to leadership.


YOU'LL BE A GREAT FIT IF YOU…

 - Have hands-on experience in messaging, CPaaS, or telecom compliance, with direct ownership of international, WhatsApp, or RCS programs - not adjacent exposure, and not a small slice of a broader ops job. Industry experience is required; we need someone who can ramp in weeks, not quarters.

 - Know the WhatsApp Business Platform from the inside: template categories, the review and approval process, quality ratings, and the common reasons templates get declined - because you've worked through them, not just read the docs.

 - Have taken RCS agents through launch: agent setup and brand verification, carrier and regional approvals, use-case categorization, and RBM content and consent policies. You know where launches stall and how to get a stuck agent moving.

 - Understand international A2P messaging: sender ID registration, country-specific documentation and pre-registration, number types, and regional carrier and regulatory rules.

 - Are comfortable working directly with carriers, aggregators, and platform partners on registrations and escalations.

 - Communicate clearly and turn complex requirements into plain-language guidance for customers and internal teams.

 - Are organized and detail-oriented, able to manage dozens of concurrent registrations, reviews, and agent launches without dropping threads.


A NOTE ON FIT: THIS IS A PRACTITIONER ROLE. IF YOUR COMPLIANCE BACKGROUND IS MOSTLY ADVISORY OR POLICY-LEVEL, OR IF WHATSAPP

source_jobs.title: Create Your Own
source_jobs.description:
TO BE CONSIDERED, INCLUDE YOUR PROPOSED JOB TITLE IN YOUR APPLICATION SUBMISSION, OR EMAIL CAREERS@SENT.DM DESCRIBING YOUR PREFERRED ROLE.




CREATE YOUR OWN ROLE

Don't see a role that perfectly fits your background? We're always open to meeting exceptional people who can help us build the future of messaging infrastructure.

We believe the best teams are built by being opportunistic about talent. If you're passionate about what we're building and have skills that could accelerate our growth, we want to hear from you - even if we aren’t specifically hiring for a position you could see yourself in.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven Excellence: A track record of exceptional work in your domain

 - Builder Mentality: You see opportunities and create solutions

 - Startup DNA: Comfortable with ambiguity and excited by rapid growth

 - Clear Impact: You can articulate exactly how you'd add value to Sent

 - Technical Appreciation: Even if not an engineer, you understand and respect technical products



OUR COMMITMENT TO YOU

 - Meaningful Equity: Competitive ownership stake with high growth potential

 - Premium Benefits: Top-tier health, dental, and vision insurance fully covered

 - 401(k) Match: We invest in your future - 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO: We trust you to manage your time and recharge when needed

 - Growth Budget: Annual allowance for courses, conferences, and learning resources

 - NYC Office: Beautiful workspace with daily meals and unlimited snacks

 - Equipment: Brand New MacBook Pro and Apple Studio Display

 - Fitness Stipend: Comped Wellhub Gold Subscription or Equinox Membership
   
   


ABOUT SENT

At Sent, we're transforming the way businesses reach and communicate with their customers. Sent is a unified messaging API that intelligently routes messages across SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and other channels. Our technology automatically identifies which channel a consumer prefers, enabling global reach while reducing the cost of messaging by 90% compared to traditional solutions.

Backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments, we recently completed a $5.1M Seed round and are rapidly scaling our platform to enable the next generation of business messaging.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer (.NET)
source_jobs.description:
Pristina, Kosovo · In-Office

About Sent

Sent is building the messaging infrastructure that powers how businesses communicate with their customers. We provide a unified API for SMS, WhatsApp, iMessage, and RCS, making it simple for developers to reach people on any channel. We've raised $5.8M in funding and we're growing fast.



The Role

We're looking for a Backend Engineer to build and maintain the core services that power Sent's messaging infrastructure. You'll work on the APIs, integrations, and backend systems that thousands of developers depend on to send millions of messages every day.



What You'll Do

• Build and maintain RESTful APIs. Design and ship the backend services that power Sent's multi-channel messaging platform. Write clean, well-tested code that's reliable at scale.

• Own system reliability. Take responsibility for the uptime and performance of the services you build. When things break, you fix them.

• Integrate across the stack. Connect Sent's platform to carrier networks, third-party providers, and internal services. Work with frontend and DevOps engineers to deliver end-to-end features.

• Improve performance. Identify bottlenecks, optimize queries, and evolve database schemas as the product scales.

• Contribute to architecture. Participate in technical design discussions and help shape how we build secure, scalable backend systems.

What We're Looking For

• 3+ years with C# / .NET / ASP.NET Core. You've built and shipped production backend services in this stack. You're comfortable with the ecosystem.

• Strong RESTful API experience. You know how to design clean APIs, version them properly, and make them easy for developers to work with.

• Solid database knowledge. You've worked with relational databases — designing schemas, writing efficient queries, thinking about data integrity.

• Security and reliability mindset. You think about authentication, authorization, and failure modes when you write code — not after.

• High-availability experience is a strong advantage. If you've built systems that need to stay up, that background will serve you well here.



Why Sent

We're a team building hard technical problems in messaging infrastructure. We value directness, craftsmanship, and actually shipping things. In Pristina, you'll be part of a close-knit engineering and operations team doing real work — not support work, not maintenance work. Work that matters. If you want to join a fast-moving company with a clear product and strong backing, this is that opportunity.



Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

source_jobs.title: Demand Generation
source_jobs.description:
About Sent

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.

We recently closed a $5.1M Seed round backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments. We're hiring to build the best infrastructure to replace multi-channel messaging stacks.

The Role

We're hiring a Demand Gen expert to build the engine that turns Sent's growing developer mindshare into signups, activated accounts, and real pipeline. The job is to own the full path to signup and live production.

This is a technical, experiment-driven role. You'll run a high-velocity testing cadence across the funnel, and you'll build a lot of it yourself: you write the code to pull your own numbers, you ship the landing page, and you understand the product well enough to use it. You'll be the person who knows, down to the dollar, what a qualified signup costs, which experiments moved it, and where the best developers come from. As one of our earliest marketing hires, you'll inherit the demand engine directly from our CEO and own its number moving forward.

You'll also work hand in hand with our developer relations function, turning technical content, demos, and community engagement into measurable demand. You know how DevRel actually works, how the ecosystem behaves, and how to plug into it without coming off as a marketer. Where DevRel builds trust and mindshare, you build the loops that capture and convert it.

What you'll do

 - Run a structured growth experimentation cadence: form hypotheses, ship tests across landing pages, onboarding, pricing, and paid creative, read the results honestly, and double down on what wins

 - Own the self-serve activation funnel end to end: signup → first API call → first production send. Instrument it, find where developers drop off, and write the experiments that fix it

 - Partner closely with DevRel to turn technical content, demos, talks, and community presence into distribution and demand, and build the measurement to show what's landing

 - Engage credibly in the developer ecosystem, Hacker News, X, Reddit, Discord, GitHub, dev newsletters and podcasts, and turn that engagement into repeatable acquisition channels

 - Own full-funnel demand across paid (search, social, podcast/newsletter sponsorships), SEO, lifecycle, and events, building repeatable systems and growth loops rather than one-off campaigns

 - Build your own tooling and tracking: write SQL against the warehouse, stand up landing pages, script automations, integrate the marketing stack via API, and build the occasional interactive demo or programmatic-SEO play without waiting on engineering

 - Own marketing's contribution to revenue, self-serve and sales-assisted, and report cleanly on CAC, conversion rates, payback, and experiment ROI

 - Partner with sales on lead scoring, handoff, and targeted account-based plays for larger logos, and with DevRel and content to turn technical work into distribution

What you bring

 - At least 4 years in demand generation or growth marketing, with experience owning a funnel or a number end to end, ideally at a developer-first, API, or infrastructure company

 - You've carried a pipeline or self-serve revenue target and hit it, and you can show which experiments got you there

 - Experience working alongside DevRel or developer marketing, and a real feel for the developer ecosystem, how communities behave, what earns credibility, and what gets you ignored

 - You code. Deeply technical skillset is expected

 - Experimentation rigor: you think in hypotheses and growth loops, you can design a clean test, and you know when a result is real versus noise

 - You know how to market to developers

 - Experience with messaging API, telecom, and API infrastructure is a big plus but n

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SENT

Sent is an infrastructure for sending A2P messages across SMS, WhatsApp, and RCS through a single unified API. Our intelligent routing model automates channel selection based on deliverability and cost, ensuring lower cost and global reach without any channel-specific integrations.

We recently closed a $5.1M Seed round backed by Companyon Ventures, Bessemer Venture Partners, UIF, and CEAS Investments. We're hiring to build the best infrastructure to replace multi-channel messaging stacks.

About the Role

Sent is replacing the messaging infrastructure that every developer complains about. We're growing into a market where the incumbents have spent years giving customers reasons to leave. We need someone to help them.

This is a closing role. You'll own the full cycle - find the opportunity, run the process, negotiate the contract, get it signed. We have a product that sells well when it gets in front of the right people, and we need someone resourceful enough to get it there.

You'll sell to engineering leaders, product teams, and founders at companies that send messages their users actually need to receive - authentication codes, delivery notifications, appointment reminders, alerts. These aren't nice-to-haves. When messaging breaks, their product breaks. That's your wedge.

The sales org is early. You'll work under our VP of Sales and CEO, and what you learn in the field will directly shape how we position, price, and package. If you want a role where your fingerprints are on the revenue number and the strategy behind it, this is it.

In this role, you will…

 - Own the full sales cycle end-to-end: prospecting, discovery, demo, negotiation, close.

 - Build your own pipeline. Outbound, inbound, events, referrals, creative plays - whatever works, you'll find it and run it.

 - Sell to technical buyers and earn their trust. You don't need to write code, but you need to understand why an API matters and what makes one better than another.

 - Run product demos that are sharp, relevant, and tailored - not scripted walkthroughs.

 - Work cross-functionally with Developer Success, Growth, and Engineering to move deals through technical evaluation and integration.

 - Keep a clean pipeline. Accurate forecasting, honest deal stages, and no sandbagging.

 - Feed market signal back into the company - what you hear from prospects should influence product, pricing, and positioning.

You'll be a great fit if you…

 - Have 3–6 years of full-cycle closing experience in mid-market/ SMB B2B SaaS with consistent quota attainment.

 - Have sold to developers, engineering managers, or technical decision-makers and can speak their language without faking it.

 - Build pipeline without being asked. You don't wait for marketing to fill your funnel.

 - Move fast. You follow up the same day, you don't let deals stall, and you treat urgency as a default setting.

 - Are low-ego and high-output.

 - Want to be early at a company where the equity is real, the comp is uncapped, and your contribution is impossible to miss.

Nice to have

 - Experience selling APIs, messaging APIs, CPaaS, developer tools, or infrastructure

 - Time at a seed or Series A company where you helped build the sales motion, not just execute one.

 - Enough technical fluency to navigate API docs or hold your own in a Slack channel with engineers.

Our Commitment to You

 - Meaningful equity with high growth potential

 - Top-tier medical, dental, vision fully covered

 - 401(k) with 100% match up to 4%

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful NYC workspace with daily meals and unlimited snacks

 - New MacBook Pro + Apple Studio Display

 - Wellhub Gold or Equinox Stipend

Sent, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences.

notes: Sent is a developer-first company powering messaging across SMS, WhatsApp, RCS, and more.
